question_text: Which of the following best describes the difference between a real image and a virtual image?

options.label: A
options.text: Real images are always larger and virtual images are always smaller

options.label: B
options.text: Real images are formed where light rays actually meet; virtual images appear to come from a point where rays don't actually meet

options.label: C
options.text: Real images can only be formed by mirrors and virtual images only by lenses

options.label: D
options.text: Real images are upright and virtual images are inverted

explanation: The key difference is whether light rays actually converge at a point. In a real image, light really meets at a point and can be projected. In a virtual image, the light rays only appear to come from a point when you trace them backwards, like looking in a mirror.
